description: Agent-friendly Context.dev CLI for website intelligence, brand enrichment, scraping, crawling, structured extraction, screenshots, styleguides, competitor maps, source packs, and change digests.

tools.name: brand_create
tools.description: Signal that you may fetch brand data for a particular domain soon to improve latency. Required: domain. Optional: timeoutMS. Returns the new BrandCreateResponse.

params.name: domain
params.description: Domain name to prefetch brand data for

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_create-ai
tools.description: Given a single URL, determines if it is a product page and extracts the product information. Required: url. Optional: maxAgeMs (default: 6.048e+08), timeoutMS. Returns the new BrandCreateAiResponse.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Return a cached result if a prior scrape for the same parameters exists and is younger than this many milliseconds. Defaults to 7 days (604800000 ms) when omitted. Max is 30 days (2592000000 ms). Set to 0 to always scrape fresh.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

params.name: url
params.description: The product page URL to extract product data from.

tools.name: brand_create-ai-2
tools.description: Extract product information from a brand's website. We will analyze the website and return a list of products with details such as name, description, image, pricing, features, and more. Returns array of BrandCreateAi2Item.

tools.name: brand_create-ai-3
tools.description: Use AI to extract specific data points from a brand's website. The AI will crawl the website and extract the requested information based on the provided data points. Required: data_to_extract, domain. Optional: specific_pages, timeoutMS. Returns the new BrandCreateAi3Response.

params.name: data_to_extract
params.description: Array of data points to extract from the website

params.name: domain
params.description: The domain name to analyze

params.name: specific-pages-about-us
params.description: Whether to analyze the about us page

params.name: specific-pages-blog
params.description: Whether to analyze the blog

params.name: specific-pages-careers
params.description: Whether to analyze the careers page

params.name: specific-pages-contact-us
params.description: Whether to analyze the contact us page

params.name: specific-pages-faq
params.description: Whether to analyze the FAQ page

params.name: specific-pages-home-page
params.description: Whether to analyze the home page

params.name: specific-pages-pricing
params.description: Whether to analyze the pricing page

params.name: specific-pages-privacy-policy
params.description: Whether to analyze the privacy policy page

params.name: specific-pages-terms-and-conditions
params.description: Whether to analyze the terms and conditions page

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_create-prefetchbyemail
tools.description: Signal that you may fetch brand data for a particular domain soon to improve latency. This endpoint accepts an email address, extracts the domain from it, validates that it's not a disposable or free email provider, and queues the domain for prefetching. Required: email. Optional: timeoutMS. Returns the new BrandCreatePrefetchbyemailResponse.

params.name: email
params.description: Email address to prefetch brand data for. The domain will be extracted from the email. Free email providers (gmail.com, yahoo.com, etc.) and disposable email addresses are not allowed.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_list
tools.description: Retrieve logos, backdrops, colors, industry, description, and more from any domain. Required: domain. Optional: force_language, maxSpeed, maxAgeMs (default: 7.776e+09) (plus 1 more). Returns the BrandResponse.

params.name: domain
params.description: Domain name to retrieve brand data for (e.g., 'example.com', 'google.com'). Cannot be used with name or ticker parameters.

params.name: force_language
params.description: Optional parameter to force the language of the retrieved brand data.

params.name: maxSpeed
params.description: Optional parameter to optimize the API call for maximum speed. When set to true, the API will skip time-consuming operations for faster response at the cost of less comprehensive data. Works with all three lookup methods.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for cached brand data before the API performs a hard refresh.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_list-retrievebyemail
tools.description: Retrieve brand information using an email address while detecting disposable and free email addresses. Disposable and free email addresses (like gmail.com, yahoo.com) will throw a 422 error. Required: email. Optional: force_language, maxSpeed, maxAgeMs (default: 7.776e+09) (plus 1 more). Returns the BrandResponse.

params.name: email
params.description: Email address to retrieve brand data for (e.g., 'contact@example.com'). The domain will be extracted from the email. Free email providers (gmail.com, yahoo.com, etc.) and disposable email addresses are not allowed.

params.name: force_language
params.description: Optional parameter to force the language of the retrieved brand data.

params.name: maxSpeed
params.description: Optional parameter to optimize the API call for maximum speed.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for cached brand data before the API performs a hard refresh.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_list-retrievebyisin
tools.description: Retrieve brand information using an ISIN (International Securities Identification Number). Required: isin. Optional: force_language, maxSpeed, maxAgeMs (default: 7.776e+09) (plus 1 more). Returns the BrandResponse.

params.name: isin
params.description: ISIN (International Securities Identification Number) to retrieve brand data for (e.g., 'AU000000IMD5', 'US0378331005'). Must be exactly 12 characters: 2 letters followed by 9 alphanumeric characters and ending with a digit.

params.name: force_language
params.description: Optional parameter to force the language of the retrieved brand data.

params.name: maxSpeed
params.description: Optional parameter to optimize the API call for maximum speed.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for cached brand data before the API performs a hard refresh.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_list-retrievebyname
tools.description: Retrieve brand information using a company name. Required: name. Optional: country_gl, force_language, maxSpeed (plus 2 more). Returns the BrandResponse.

params.name: name
params.description: Company name to retrieve brand data for (e.g., 'Apple Inc', 'Microsoft Corporation'). Must be 3-30 characters.

params.name: country_gl
params.description: Optional country code hint (GL parameter) to specify the country for the company name.

params.name: force_language
params.description: Optional parameter to force the language of the retrieved brand data.

params.name: maxSpeed
params.description: Optional parameter to optimize the API call for maximum speed.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for cached brand data before the API performs a hard refresh.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_list-retrievebyticker
tools.description: Retrieve brand information using a stock ticker symbol. Required: ticker. Optional: ticker_exchange, force_language, maxSpeed (plus 2 more). Returns the BrandResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Stock ticker symbol to retrieve brand data for (e.g., 'AAPL', 'GOOGL', 'BRK.A'). Must be 1-15 characters, letters/numbers/dots only.

params.name: ticker_exchange
params.description: Optional stock exchange for the ticker. Defaults to NASDAQ if not specified.

params.name: force_language
params.description: Optional parameter to force the language of the retrieved brand data.

params.name: maxSpeed
params.description: Optional parameter to optimize the API call for maximum speed.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for cached brand data before the API performs a hard refresh.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_list-retrievesimplified
tools.description: Returns a simplified version of brand data containing only essential information: domain, title, colors, logos, and backdrops. Optimized for faster responses and reduced data transfer. Required: domain. Optional: maxAgeMs (default: 7.776e+09), timeoutMS.

params.name: domain
params.description: Domain name to retrieve simplified brand data for

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for cached brand data before the API performs a hard refresh.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: brand_list-transactionidentifier
tools.description: Endpoint specially designed for platforms that want to identify transaction data by the transaction title. Required: transaction_info. Optional: country_gl, city, mcc (plus 5 more). Returns the BrandResponse.

params.name: transaction_info
params.description: Transaction information to identify the brand

params.name: country_gl
params.description: Optional country code (GL parameter) to specify the country. This affects the geographic location used for search queries.

params.name: city
params.description: Optional city name to prioritize when searching for the brand.

params.name: mcc
params.description: Optional Merchant Category Code (MCC) to help identify the business category/industry.

params.name: phone
params.description: Optional phone number from the transaction to help verify brand match.

params.name: high_confidence_only
params.description: When set to true, the API will perform an additional verification steps to ensure the identified brand matches the transaction with high confidence.

params.name: force_language
params.description: Optional parameter to force the language of the retrieved brand data.

params.name: maxSpeed
params.description: Optional parameter to optimize the API call for maximum speed.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: people_create
tools.description: Retrieve and normalize a person profile from identifiers. Required: identifiers. Optional: timeoutMS. Returns the new PersonRetrieveResponse.

params.name: identifiers-linkedin-url
params.description: LinkedIn profile URL, e.g. https://www.linkedin.com/in/yahia-bakour/.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_create
tools.description: Performs a crawl starting from a given URL, extracts page content as Markdown, and returns results for all crawled pages. Required: url. Optional: excludeSelectors, followSubdomains (default: false), includeFrames (default: false) (plus 13 more).

params.name: excludeSelectors
params.description: CSS selectors to remove before each crawled page is converted to Markdown. Applied after includeSelectors. Exclusion takes precedence: an element matching both is removed. Examples: 'nav', 'footer', '.ad-banner', '[aria-hidden=true]'.

params.name: followSubdomains
params.description: When true, follow links on subdomains of the starting URL's domain (e.g. docs.example.com when starting from example.com). www and apex are always treated as equivalent.

params.name: includeFrames
params.description: When true, the contents of iframes are rendered to Markdown for each crawled page.

params.name: includeImages
params.description: Include image references in the Markdown output

params.name: includeLinks
params.description: Preserve hyperlinks in the Markdown output

params.name: includeSelectors
params.description: CSS selectors. When provided, only matching HTML subtrees (and their descendants) are kept before each crawled page is converted to Markdown. When omitted, the entire document is kept. Examples: 'article.main', '#content', '[role=main]'.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Return a cached result if a prior scrape for the same parameters exists and is younger than...

params.name: maxDepth
params.description: Maximum link depth from the starting URL (0 = only the starting page)

params.name: maxPages
params.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl. Hard cap: 500.

params.name: pdf-end
params.description: Last 1-based PDF page to parse. When omitted, parsing ends at the last page. Must be greater than or equal to start when both are provided.

params.name: pdf-should-parse
params.description: When true, PDF pages are fetched and parsed. When false, PDF pages are skipped entirely (not included in results and not counted as failures).

params.name: pdf-start
params.description: First 1-based PDF page to parse. When omitted, parsing starts at the first page.

params.name: shortenBase64Images
params.description: Truncate base64-encoded image data in the Markdown output

params.name: stopAfterMs
params.description: Soft time budget for the crawl in milliseconds. After each scrape, the crawler checks the elapsed time and, if exceeded, returns the pages collected so far instead of continuing. Min: 10000 (10s). Max: 110000 (110s). Default: 80000 (80s).

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

params.name: url
params.description: The starting URL for the crawl (must include http:// or https:// protocol)

params.name: urlRegex
params.description: Regex pattern. Only URLs matching this pattern will be followed and scraped.

params.name: useMainContentOnly
params.description: Extract only the main content, stripping headers, footers, sidebars, and navigation

params.name: waitForMs
params.description: Optional browser wait time in milliseconds after initial page load for each crawled page. Min: 0. Max: 30000 (30 seconds).

tools.name: web_create-extract
tools.description: Crawl a website, use the provided JSON Schema and instructions to prioritize relevant internal links, and extract structured data from the selected pages. Required: schema, url. Optional: factCheck (default: false), followSubdomains (default: false), includeFrames (default: false) (plus 8 more). Returns array of string.

params.name: factCheck
params.description: When true, every returned value must be grounded in facts stated on the page; fields that cannot be supported by the page are returned as null/empty. When false (default), the model may make reasonable inferences and derivations from the page content (e.g. ideal customer, competitor analysis, recommendations) while keeping verifiable specifics (names, quotes, URLs, dates, metrics) faithful to the source.

params.name: followSubdomains
params.description: When true, follow links on subdomains of the starting URL's domain.

params.name: includeFrames
params.description: When true, iframe contents are included in Markdown before extraction.

params.name: instructions
params.description: Optional extraction guidance, such as which facts to prioritize or how to interpret fields in the schema.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Return cached scrape results if a prior scrape for the same parameters is younger than this many milliseconds. Defaults to 7 days (604800000 ms).

params.name: maxDepth
params.description: Optional maximum link depth from the starting URL (0 = only the starting page). If omitted, there is no crawl depth limit.

params.name: maxPages
params.description: Maximum number of pages to analyze for extraction. Hard cap: 50. Defaults to 5.

params.name: pdf-end
params.description: Last 1-based PDF page to parse. Must be greater than or equal to start when both are provided.

params.name: pdf-should-parse
params.description: When true, PDF pages are fetched and parsed. When false, PDF pages are skipped.

params.name: pdf-start
params.description: First 1-based PDF page to parse.

params.name: schema
params.description: JSON Schema for the returned data object. TypeScript Zod users can pass a JSON Schema generated from a Zod object; Python users can pass the equivalent JSON Schema object.

params.name: stopAfterMs
params.description: Soft time budget for the crawl in milliseconds. Min: 10000 (10s). Max: 110000 (110s). Default: 80000 (80s).

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

params.name: url
params.description: The starting website URL to crawl and extract from. Must include http:// or https://.

params.name: waitForMs
params.description: Optional browser wait time in milliseconds after initial page load for each crawled page.

tools.name: web_create-search
tools.description: Search the web and optionally scrape each result to Markdown in one round-trip. Required: query. Optional: excludeDomains, freshness, includeDomains (plus 3 more). Returns array of WebCreateSearchItem.

params.name: excludeDomains
params.description: Blocklist — drop results from these domains. Example: ['pinterest.com', 'reddit.com'].

params.name: freshness
params.description: Restrict results to content published within this window.

params.name: includeDomains
params.description: Allowlist — only return results from these domains. Example: ['arxiv.org', 'github.com'].

params.name: markdown-options-enabled
params.description: Scrape each result to Markdown. Off by default to keep search cheap and fast.

params.name: markdown-options-include-frames
params.description: Render iframe contents into the Markdown.

params.name: markdown-options-include-images
params.description: Emit image references in the Markdown.

params.name: markdown-options-include-links
params.description: Keep hyperlinks in the Markdown.

params.name: markdown-options-max-age-ms
params.description: Cache TTL in ms for scraped Markdown keyed by URL + options. Default 1 day, max 30 days. Set to 0 to force a fresh scrape.

params.name: markdown-options-pdf-end
params.description: Last PDF page to parse (1-based, inclusive). Defaults to the final page. Must be >= start.

params.name: markdown-options-pdf-should-parse
params.description: Parse PDF URLs. When false, PDF results are skipped with WEBSITE_ACCESS_ERROR.

params.name: markdown-options-pdf-start
params.description: First PDF page to parse (1-based, inclusive). Defaults to page 1.

params.name: markdown-options-shorten-base64-images
params.description: Truncate inline base64 image payloads to keep responses small.

params.name: markdown-options-timeout-ms
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

params.name: markdown-options-use-main-content-only
params.description: Strip nav, header, footer, and sidebar — keep only the primary article content.

params.name: markdown-options-wait-for-ms
params.description: Extra wait after page load before rendering, in ms (0–30000). Useful for JS-heavy pages.

params.name: query
params.description: Natural-language search query.

params.name: queryFanout
params.description: Expand the query into multiple parallel variants for broader recall.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list
tools.description: Analyze a company's landing page and web search evidence to return direct competitors for the same product or market. Required: domain. Optional: numCompetitors (default: 5), timeoutMS. Returns array of WebListItem.

params.name: domain
params.description: Company domain to analyze, such as `stripe.com`. Full http(s) URLs are accepted and normalized to their domain.

params.name: numCompetitors
params.description: Exact number of direct competitors to return. Defaults to 5.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-fonts
tools.description: Scrape font information from a website including font families, usage statistics, fallbacks, and element/word counts. Optional: domain, directUrl, maxAgeMs (default: 7.776e+09) (plus 1 more). Returns array of WebListFontsItem.

params.name: domain
params.description: Domain name to extract fonts from (e.g., 'example.com', 'google.com'). The domain will be automatically normalized and validated. You must provide either 'domain' or 'directUrl', but not both.

params.name: directUrl
params.description: A specific URL to fetch fonts from directly, bypassing domain resolution (e.g., 'https://example.com/design-system'). When provided, fonts are extracted from this exact URL. You must provide either 'domain' or 'directUrl', but not both.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for cached data before the API performs a hard refresh. Defaults to 3 months (7776000000 ms). Values below 1 day (86400000 ms) are clamped to 1 day; values above 1 year (31536000000 ms) are clamped to 1 year.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-naics
tools.description: Classify any brand into 2022 NAICS industry codes from its domain or name. Required: input. Optional: minResults (default: 1), maxResults (default: 5), timeoutMS. Returns array of WebListNaicsItem.

params.name: input
params.description: Brand domain or title to retrieve NAICS code for. If a valid domain is provided, it will be used for classification, otherwise, we will search for the brand using the provided title.

params.name: minResults
params.description: Minimum number of NAICS codes to return. Must be at least 1. Defaults to 1.

params.name: maxResults
params.description: Maximum number of NAICS codes to return. Must be between 1 and 10. Defaults to 5.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-scrape
tools.description: Scrapes the given URL and returns the raw HTML content of the page. Required: url. Optional: pdf (default: map[shouldParse:true]), includeFrames (default: false), useMainContentOnly (default: false) (plus 6 more).

params.name: url
params.description: Full URL to scrape (must include http:// or https:// protocol)

params.name: pdf
params.description: PDF parsing controls. Use start/end to limit text extraction and OCR to an inclusive 1-based...

params.name: includeFrames
params.description: When true, iframes are rendered inline into the returned HTML.

params.name: useMainContentOnly
params.description: When true, return only the page's main content in the HTML response, excluding headers, footers, sidebars, and navigation when detectable.

params.name: includeSelectors
params.description: CSS selectors. When provided, only matching subtrees (and their descendants) are kept and everything else is dropped. When omitted, the entire document is kept. Examples: 'article.main', '#content', '[role=main]'.

params.name: excludeSelectors
params.description: CSS selectors to remove from the result. Applied after includeSelectors. Exclusion takes precedence: an element matching both is removed. Examples: 'nav', 'footer', '.ad-banner', '[aria-hidden=true]'.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Return a cached result if a prior scrape for the same parameters exists and is younger than...

params.name: waitForMs
params.description: Optional browser wait time in milliseconds after initial page load. Min: 0. Max: 30000 (30 seconds).

params.name: headers
params.description: Optional outbound HTTP headers forwarded only to the target URL, sent as deep-object query...

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-scrape-2
tools.description: Extract image assets from a web page, including standard URLs, inline SVGs, data URIs, responsive image sources, metadata, CSS backgrounds, video posters, and embeds. The base request costs 1 credit. When enrichment is enabled, the entire call costs 5 credits. Required: url. Optional: maxAgeMs (default: 8.64e+07), enrichment, waitForMs (plus 2 more). Returns array of WebListScrape2Item.

params.name: url
params.description: Page URL to inspect. Must include http:// or https://.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Reuse a cached result this many milliseconds old or newer. Default: 86400000 (1 day). Set to 0 to bypass cache. Maximum: 2592000000 (30 days).

params.name: enrichment
params.description: Optional per-image processing, sent as deep-object query params such as enrichment[resolution]=true.

params.name: waitForMs
params.description: Optional browser wait time in milliseconds after initial page load before collecting images. Min: 0. Max: 30000 (30 seconds).

params.name: headers
params.description: Optional outbound HTTP headers forwarded only to the target URL, sent as deep-object query...

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-scrape-3
tools.description: Scrapes the given URL into LLM usable Markdown. Required: url. Optional: includeLinks (default: true), includeImages (default: false), shortenBase64Images (default: true) (plus 9 more). Returns the WebListScrape3Response.

params.name: url
params.description: Full URL to scrape into LLM usable Markdown (must include http:// or https:// protocol)

params.name: includeLinks
params.description: Preserve hyperlinks in Markdown output

params.name: includeImages
params.description: Include image references in Markdown output

params.name: shortenBase64Images
params.description: Shorten base64-encoded image data in the Markdown output

params.name: useMainContentOnly
params.description: Extract only the main content of the page, excluding headers, footers, sidebars, and navigation

params.name: pdf
params.description: PDF parsing controls. Use start/end to limit text extraction and OCR to an inclusive 1-based...

params.name: includeFrames
params.description: When true, the contents of iframes are rendered to Markdown.

params.name: includeSelectors
params.description: CSS selectors. When provided, only matching HTML subtrees (and their descendants) are kept before conversion to Markdown. When omitted, the entire document is kept. Examples: 'article.main', '#content', '[role=main]'.

params.name: excludeSelectors
params.description: CSS selectors to remove before conversion to Markdown. Applied after includeSelectors. Exclusion takes precedence: an element matching both is removed. Examples: 'nav', 'footer', '.ad-banner', '[aria-hidden=true]'.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Return a cached result if a prior scrape for the same parameters exists and is younger than...

params.name: waitForMs
params.description: Optional browser wait time in milliseconds after initial page load before converting the page to Markdown. Min: 0. Max: 30000 (30 seconds).

params.name: headers
params.description: Optional outbound HTTP headers forwarded only to the target URL, sent as deep-object query...

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-scrape-4
tools.description: Crawl an entire website's sitemap and return all discovered page URLs. Required: domain. Optional: maxLinks (default: 10000), urlRegex, headers (plus 1 more). Returns array of string.

params.name: domain
params.description: Domain to build a sitemap for

params.name: maxLinks
params.description: Maximum number of links to return from the sitemap crawl. Defaults to 10,000. Minimum is 1, maximum is 100,000.

params.name: urlRegex
params.description: Optional RE2-compatible regex pattern. Only URLs matching this pattern are returned and counted against maxLinks.

params.name: headers
params.description: Optional outbound HTTP headers forwarded only to the target URL, sent as deep-object query...

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of a website. Optional: domain, directUrl, fullScreenshot (plus 6 more). Returns the WebListScreenshotResponse.

params.name: domain
params.description: Domain name to take screenshot of (e.g., 'example.com', 'google.com'). The domain will be automatically normalized and validated. You must provide either 'domain' or 'directUrl', but not both.

params.name: directUrl
params.description: A specific URL to screenshot directly, bypassing domain resolution (e.g., 'https://example.com/pricing'). When provided, the screenshot is taken of this exact URL. You must provide either 'domain' or 'directUrl', but not both.

params.name: fullScreenshot
params.description: Optional parameter to determine screenshot type. If 'true', takes a full page screenshot capturing all content. If 'false' or not provided, takes a viewport screenshot (standard browser view).

params.name: handleCookiePopup
params.description: Optional parameter to control cookie/consent popup handling. If 'true', we dismiss cookie banner before capture. If 'false' or not provided, captures the page without that step.

params.name: viewport
params.description: Optional browser viewport dimensions for the screenshot. Defaults to 1920x1080.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Return a cached screenshot if a prior screenshot for the same parameters exists and is younger than this many milliseconds. Defaults to 1 day (86400000 ms) when omitted. Max is 30 days (2592000000 ms). Set to 0 to always capture fresh.

params.name: page
params.description: Optional parameter to specify which page type to screenshot. If provided, the system will scrape the domain's links and use heuristics to find the most appropriate URL for the specified page type (30 supported languages). If not provided, screenshots the main domain landing page. Only applicable when using 'domain', not 'directUrl'.

params.name: waitForMs
params.description: Optional browser wait time in milliseconds after initial page load before taking the screenshot. Min: 0. Max: 30000 (30 seconds). Defaults to 3000 ms when omitted.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-sic
tools.description: Classify any brand into Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) codes from its domain or name. Choose between the original SIC system (`original_sic`) or the latest SIC list maintained by the SEC (`latest_sec`). Required: input. Optional: type (default: original_sic), minResults (default: 1), maxResults (default: 5) (plus 1 more). Returns array of WebListSicItem.

params.name: input
params.description: Brand domain or title to retrieve SIC code for. If a valid domain is provided, it will be used for classification, otherwise, we will search for the brand using the provided title.

params.name: type
params.description: Which SIC dataset to classify against. `original_sic` uses the 1987 Standard Industrial Classification system; `latest_sec` uses the current SIC list as published by the SEC. Defaults to `original_sic`.

params.name: minResults
params.description: Minimum number of SIC codes to return. Must be at least 1. Defaults to 1.

params.name: maxResults
params.description: Maximum number of SIC codes to return. Must be between 1 and 10. Defaults to 5.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.

tools.name: web_list-styleguide
tools.description: Extract a comprehensive design system from a website including colors, typography, spacing, shadows, and UI components. Optional: domain, directUrl, maxAgeMs (default: 7.776e+09) (plus 1 more). Returns the WebListStyleguideResponse.

params.name: domain
params.description: Domain name to extract styleguide from (e.g., 'example.com', 'google.com'). The domain will be automatically normalized and validated. You must provide either 'domain' or 'directUrl', but not both.

params.name: directUrl
params.description: A specific URL to fetch the styleguide from directly, bypassing domain resolution (e.g., 'https://example.com/design-system'). When provided, the styleguide is extracted from this exact URL. You must provide either 'domain' or 'directUrl', but not both.

params.name: maxAgeMs
params.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for cached data before the API performs a hard refresh. Defaults to 3 months (7776000000 ms). Values below 1 day (86400000 ms) are clamped to 1 day; values above 1 year (31536000000 ms) are clamped to 1 year.

params.name: timeoutMS
params.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for the request.
